expected_properties: After reset deassertion, out updates on each rising clk edge following XOR(out[3], out[2]) inverted feedback rule.
out never becomes X during observation window.
additional_cases.goal: Validate correct LFSR state transition on clock edges after reset.
preconditions: Reset has been applied and released.
stimulus_intent: Assert reset high, then release it, then generate a sequence of rising clock edges.
timing_assumptions: Observe at least 4 consecutive rising edges after reset release.

expected_properties: When clk remains low, out retains its value across observation cycles.
additional_cases.goal: Confirm that LFSR does not change state without clock activity.
stimulus_intent: Hold clk low for multiple cycles after reset release.
timing_assumptions: No rising edge of clk occurs during the observation window.

expected_properties: Out remains defined (no X) even if reset is asserted while clk toggles.
additional_cases.goal: Assess robustness to potentially illegal reset timing.
stimulus_intent: Assert rst high while generating clock toggles.

expected_properties: out changes on each rising clk edge after reset according to LFSR polynomial.
No X values appear on out during observation.
baseline_case_enrichments.goal: Observe deterministic LFSR progression after reset.
notes: Conservative due to unknown latency; only external behavior is checked.
stimulus_intent: Release reset and observe several clock cycles.
timing_assumptions: Observe at least 5 rising edges post-reset.
